description: Four photos of a recently seeded KBG lawn in St. Paul MN showing damping-off symptoms from overwatering. Tests that the AI recognizes seedling damping-off, avoids pre-emergent/post-emergent herbicide recommendations, and addresses overwatering as the primary cause.

photos.caption: Wide view of a recently seeded lawn with patchy germination — some areas with emerging green seedlings alongside sections with collapsed or absent seedlings.
Close-up of newly germinated KBG seedlings showing healthy green shoots in some areas next to collapsed, water-soaked seedlings with discolored stems at soil level.
Soil surface view showing standing water pooling in low areas of the seeded bed, indicating overwatering or poor drainage contributing to damping-off conditions.
Pale yellowish patches amid emerging seedlings — yellowing and thinning consistent with nutrient deficiency in seedling stage or seedling decline from root rot.

profile.notes: Seeded 3 weeks ago with KBG. Germination started but some patches are collapsing. Have been watering 4-5 times daily to keep soil moist. Some yellow areas developing.
profile.currentRoutine: Watering very frequently — 4 to 5 times daily trying to keep the seed moist. No fertilizer applied yet to seedlings.

groundTruth.photoNotes: Four photos of a recently seeded KBG lawn with damping-off. Wide shot shows patchy germination with missing areas. Close-up reveals collapsed seedlings with water-soaked stems at the crown. Pooling water visible in low areas. Yellow patches suggest early nutrient deficiency or secondary root rot. Classic overwatering-induced damping-off presentation.
